rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in the correct AADL scope: component type annexes for interface-level assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for deployment bindings.
Deployment bindings and assignments must be placed in the implementation annex, not the component type annex.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports must be placed in valid component type scopes.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
Component type contracts may reference only features visible at that type scope; implementation-level references must be in the implementation annex.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Subcomponent ports should not be used in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes; use implementation annexes for such clauses.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assume/guarantee syntax and validator diagnostics
